vulnerability.reference: LLMs fine-tuned on biased preference data amplify societal stereotypes in user interactions.
vulnerability.generated: Users cannot detect spurious preference encoding in LLM outputs due to opaque reward distribution alignment.

impact.reference: Users receive discriminatory recommendations and content, reinforcing systemic biases.
impact.generated: Biased LLM responses systematically reinforce discriminatory preferences across user populations.

dose_response.reference: Miscalibrated distributional preference alignment embeds spurious bias in LLM rewards, causing interactions to produce discriminatory recommendations that reinforce systemic biases.
dose_response.generated: Miscalibrated or distributionally misaligned conditional transport plans, through users interacting with distributionally preference-aligned LLMs, trigger users' inability to detect spurious preference encoding from opaque reward alignment, leading to biased LLM responses systematically reinforcing discriminatory preferences across user populations.
